title: Odik II

'''Odik II''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was located on the [[Byss Run/Legends|Byss Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. It housed [[Politics/Legends|political]] [[Prison/Legends|detention wards]] used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to secretly [[Prisoner/Legends|imprison]] many dissidents. The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Calders]] served at the detention facility before being reassigned aboard the Imperial [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]].

==Description==
Odik II<ref name="DSTC2" /> was a [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]<ref name="EAOC" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Encyclopedia">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> that occupied the second [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position within<ref name="DSTC2" /> the [[Odik system/Legends|Odik system]].<ref name="TEA" /> The [[Byss Run/Legends|Byss Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] connected it to<ref name="FDCR" /> the [[Prakith system/Legends|Prakith]] and [[Beshqek system|Byss systems]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
[[File:Sector5.png|thumb|left|180px|Odik II was part of the Republic's Sector 5 theater during the Clone Wars.]]
When the first campaigns of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] were waged between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the Odik [[Star system/Legends|system]] fell within [[Sector 5/Legends|Sector 5]], an area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] that the [[Fifth Deep Core Army]] of the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]] was tasked with defending against the [[Confederacy military/Legends|military forces]] of the Confederacy.<ref name="TEGTW" /> During the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Odik II served as the seat of power for [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Cinzero Gann]].<ref name="FDCR" />

Odik II also was the site of highly-secret [[Politics/Legends|political]] [[Prison/Legends|detention wards]] used to [[Prisoner/Legends|imprison]] many Imperial dissidents. The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Calders]] began his career as a [[Security guard/Legends|security guard]] at the prison camps of Odik II. He excelled at his work and shortly became the [[Warden/Legends|warden]] for the entire detention facility.<ref name="DSTC2" /> By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], thereby providing the date by which [[Calders]] must have been reassigned to the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]].</ref> Calders had been reassigned to the Imperial [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], which was being constructed over the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] planet [[Despayre]].<ref name="DSTC2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Odik II was first mentioned in ''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]'', a [[1991]] sourcebook authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="DSTC">''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]''</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Odik system, and therefore Odik II, in grid square K-11.<ref name="TEA" />
